Maternal, Child, & Adolescent Health Home Page
About MCAH
The Division of Maternal, Child, and Adolescent Health (MCAH) supports and advocates for the
 wellbeing of infants, mothers, fathers, children, and adolescents, and their families in Los
 Angeles County.
Vision
All women, men, infants, children, adolescents, and their families in Los
 Angeles County will thrive in a safe, healthy, and nurturing environment.
Mission
All pregnant and postpartum people in Los Angeles County receive enhanced
 perinatal health services through an integrated system designed to optimize
 maternal and child health outcomes.
Find Support Today
Programs and Resources Available
The Maternal, Child, and Adolescent Health (MCAH) Division envisions a safe,
 healthy, and nurturing environment for all women, infants, children, adolescents,
 and families in Los Angeles County. Its major programs include:
Prenatal and
 Postpartum Health
Child Health
Adolescent Health

Prenatal and Postpartum Health
Supporting the health and well-being of birthing people, infants, and families
 is essential for strong, thriving communities. Our comprehensive prenatal and
 postpartum programs are designed to provide guidance, care, and resources
 throughout pregnancy, childbirth, and the early stages of parenting.
African American Infant and Maternal Mortality
 Prevention Initiative (AAIMM)
Obtain free doula care, fatherhood support and get involved with your local
 Community Action Team.
Click here to learn more
.
AAIMM Doula Program
The AAIMM Doula Program, recognized nationally for its innovation and
 effectiveness, provides culturally competent, complimentary support to
 Black/African American pregnant residents of LA County.
Click here to learn more
.
Black Infant Health
Prenatal and postpartum services for black women 16 years or older to encourage
 healthy behaviors during pregnancy.
Click here to learn more
.
Comprehensive Perinatal Services Program
Statewide comprehensive perinatal services program. Offering technical support
 and resources to LA County providers.

Child Health
Every child deserves a healthy start in life. Our child health programs support
 families with young children by offering essential services and trusted
 information to promote safe, healthy development. From
home visiting for
 new parents
to expert guidance on how to access quality early
 learning, we are here to help caregivers during key developmental stages.
Asthma Coalition
A coalition of stakeholders advocating for policy and systems change to prevent,
 minimize and manage the burden of asthma.
Click here to learn more
.
Childhood Lead Poisoning Prevention Program
 (CLPPP)
Provides preventative education on lead poisoning and support to lead burdened
 children and their families.
Click here
 to learn more
.
Community Health Outreach Initiatives
Conducts strategic outreach in LA County to assist low-income residents seeking
 health care coverage for children.
Click here to learn more
.
Help Me Grow
Help Me Grow LA works with families to find and connect to services that can
 support their child's development.
Click here to learn more
.
Home Visiting Programs
From pregnancy through age 5, our FREE home visiting programs bring support
 right to you.
Click here to learn more
.
Safe Sleep/Suddent Infant Death Syndrome (SIDS)
Safe sleep education and grief support for families affected by infant loss.

Adolescent Health
Healthy teens grow into healthy adults. Our adolescent health initiatives are
 designed to empower youth with the knowledge, resources, and support they need
 to thrive. We engage young people directly through our
Youth Advisory
 Council
, ensuring their voices shape the programs that serve them.
Asthma Coalition
A coalition of stakeholders advocating for policy and systems change to prevent,
 minimize and manage the burden of asthma.
Click here to learn more
.
Childhood Lead Poisoning Prevention Program
 (CLPPP)
Provides preventative education on lead poisoning and support to lead burdened
 children and their families.
Click here
 to learn more
.
Youth Advisory Council
Aims to provide youth and young adults with opportunities to learn about public
 health issues and improve public health services for youth in LA County.
Click here to learn more
.
Data Resources Center
The
MCAH Data Resource Center
is designed to improve access to
 local Maternal, Child, and Adolescent Health (MCAH) data. Its goal is to offer a
 centralized, user-friendly online hub featuring a wide range of local datasets,
 interactive tools, and related data resources. This platform supports public
 health professionals, researchers, practitioners, policymakers, and students in
 making informed decisions and advancing maternal and child health efforts.
Click here to learn more
.

Black Infant Health Program Referral
Are you a Black/African American woman, age 18 or older, up to 30 weeks pregnant
 or parenting a child less than 6 months old looking for support through your
 pregnancy/postpartum journey? Consider joining the Black Infant Health Program.
 Click learn more below for full details.
